claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, let me read the official guidance to ensure I have the most accurate information for you.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, do you have any children under the age of 16, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have two children - Alex is 13 and Blake is 15.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's great to know about Alex (13) and Blake (15).

Now, do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to understand your living arrangements with your children.

Do both Alex and Blake live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me at home, but Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I need to understand the situation with Blake a bit more.

Since Blake doesn't live with you, are you paying at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount (or the equivalent in kind) towards Blake's upkeep? This could include things like food, clothes, pocket money, or financial contributions towards their accommodation.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute to Blake's upkeep. I help out with things like clothes and other essentials.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Blake accurately, I need to know a bit more about the contributions you make.

Approximately how much do you contribute towards Blake's upkeep each month (or week)? And do you know roughly what the Child Benefit amount is? For context, Child Benefit is currently £25.60 per week for the eldest/only child and £16.95 per week for additional children.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I contribute about £26 per week towards Blake's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that detail. That's very helpful.

Now I need to ask about another important aspect: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital or in care (for more than 8-12 weeks), or do they have any other special living arrangements I should know about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Blake has been in care for a short while - just about a month or so.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. That's important information. I need to understand Blake's situation a bit better.

When Blake is in care, does Blake spend at least 24 hours a week at home with you, or perhaps spend 2 nights in a row each week or 7 days or more in a row at home?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake doesn't come home that regularly while in care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

Do either Alex or Blake work for 24 hours or more per week, or receive any benefits in their own right such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them work that much or receive any benefits themselves.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Based on the information you've provided, here's my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility:

**For Alex (13):** ✅ **You are eligible**
Alex lives with you in the UK, is under 16, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Alex.

**For Blake (15):** ✅ **You are currently eligible, but this may change**
Blake has been in care for about a month (approximately 4 weeks), which is within the 8-week limit. Since you're contributing £26 per week towards Blake's upkeep (which is more than the Child Benefit amount), you can currently claim Child Benefit for Blake.

**Important note about Blake:** If Blake remains in care beyond 8 weeks total without spending at least 24 hours per week at home (or 2 consecutive nights per week at home), your Child Benefit for Blake will stop. You should contact HMRC if Blake's circumstances change or if they remain in care beyond 8 weeks to discuss your ongoing eligibility.

You can claim Child Benefit for both children at this time. If you haven't already claimed, you should apply as soon as possible. Remember to notify HMRC if there are any changes to your circumstances, particularly regarding Blake's care situation.

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week and is not receiving benefits in their own right. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex as you are responsible for them and they live with you.

Blake is 15 years old (under 16) and you are contributing approximately £26 per week towards their upkeep, which exceeds the Child Benefit amount for an additional child (£16.95 per week). However, Blake has been in care for about a month (approximately 4 weeks). According to the rules, you can continue to receive Child Benefit for a child in care for up to 8 weeks, with exceptions if the child spends at least 24 hours a week at home or 2 nights in a row each week at home. Since Blake has been in care for about a month (which is within the 8-week limit) and you are making contributions to their upkeep, you are currently still eligible for Child Benefit for Blake. However, if Blake remains in care beyond 8 weeks without regular home visits (at least 24 hours per week or 2 consecutive nights per week), your eligibility for Blake will cease unless other exceptions apply.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You have two children: Alex (13) who lives with you, and Blake (15) who has been in care for about a month. You live in the UK and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex as they live with you. For Blake, you are currently eligible because they have only been in care for about 4 weeks (within the 8-week limit) and you contribute £26 per week towards their upkeep. However, if Blake remains in care beyond 8 weeks total without spending at least 24 hours per week at home or 2 consecutive nights per week at home, your Child Benefit for Blake will stop.
